summary: PR #2611 matched and linked the item translation unit src/melee/it/items/itdosei.c, changing configure.py from NonMatching to Matching for that object. The diff primarily refactors itdosei rotation/facing-angle helper usage, removes several PAD_STACK calls, adjusts some Item access expressions from GET_ITEM(gobj) to gobj->user_data, adds an sdata2_ordering helper with volatile literals, and reorders itDosei_UnkMotion10_Phys/Coll after Anim. The bot report records one new match for main/melee/it/items/itdosei itDosei_UnkMotion4_Anim and linked-code/data progress.

key_files.role: Build/config matching status changed for melee/it/items/itdosei.c from NonMatching to Matching.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: configure.py MatchingFor hunk changes Object(NonMatching, "melee/it/items/itdosei.c") to Object(Matching, "melee/it/items/itdosei.c")

key_files.role: Item source TU matched/refactored; contains helper rewrites, stack-padding removals, rotation helper changes, and code/data ordering tactics.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev bot report records itDosei_UnkMotion4_Anim 99.23% to 100.00%

what_changed.change: Linked itdosei.c as Matching in configure.py.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: configure.py Object(NonMatching, "melee/it/items/itdosei.c") replaced with Object(Matching, "melee/it/items/itdosei.c")

what_changed.change: Removed placeholder/platform and several unused-looking includes, added it/itgroundcoll.h, and retained item, map, math, lb, and random includes needed by the rewritten source.
Added static sdata2_ordering with volatile f32/f64 constants including 1.0f, 0.5f, 0.0f, M_PI_2, M_PI/4, M_PI, 4503601774854144.0, and 60.0f.
Replaced locally expanded jobj.h-style rotation setters and asserts with calls to existing HSD_JObjSetRotationX/Y/ZWithMtxDirty helpers and a new local HSD_JObjSetRotationZeroWithMtxDirty wrapper.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removed itDosei_SetRotX/SetRotY/SetRotZ expanded assert bodies
raw_diff: added HSD_JObjSetRotationZeroWithMtxDirty and calls in itDosei_SetupWalk_FC and itDosei_UnkMotion4_Anim_inline

what_changed.change: Split facing-angle calculation into static inline f32 itDosei_FacingAngle and call HSD_JObjSetRotationY or HSD_JObjSetRotationYWithMtxDirty at use sites.
Removed multiple PAD_STACK calls from animation/collision/setup functions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removed PAD_STACK(8), PAD_STACK(16), PAD_STACK(24) in several itdosei functions

what_changed.change: Changed selected Item pointer loads from GET_ITEM(gobj) to gobj->user_data.
Added small inline wrappers for itDosei_UnkMotion4_Anim attribute and animation-speed access, and introduced an explicitly commented 'permuterslop' null-check block inside that function.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: added itDosei_UnkMotion4_GetAnimSpeed, itDosei_UnkMotion4_GetAttrs, and comment '// permuterslop'

what_changed.change: Moved itDosei_UnkMotion10_Phys and itDosei_UnkMotion10_Coll definitions after itDosei_UnkMotion10_Anim.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removed Phys/Coll before Anim and re-added after Anim

what_changed.change: Bot report recorded one new match: main/melee/it/items/itdosei itDosei_UnkMotion4_Anim from 99.23% to 100.00%, plus matched code +572 bytes, linked code +10624 bytes, linked data +320 bytes, and .sdata2 improvement from 87.50% to 96.30%.

smart_moves.move: Used existing HSD_JObjSetRotation*WithMtxDirty helpers instead of keeping copied jobj.h assert/setter bodies for X/Y/Z rotation.
smart_moves.why_it_matters: This aligns with the loaded header-inlines standard and reduces expanded inline/assert noise while preserving the mtx-dirty behavior visible in the old code.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: added calls to HSD_JObjSetRotationXWithMtxDirty, HSD_JObjSetRotationYWithMtxDirty, HSD_JObjSetRotationZWithMtxDirty

smart_moves.move: Factored the recurring facing-angle expression into itDosei_FacingAngle while leaving the actual rotation setter choice at each call site.
smart_moves.why_it_matters: The diff shows normal HSD_JObjSetRotationY calls at ordinary sites and WithMtxDirty calls where the matching shape needs dirtying behavior.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: call sites use either HSD_JObjSetRotationY or HSD_JObjSetRotationYWithMtxDirty

smart_moves.move: Removed PAD_STACK uses while still achieving the reported match for itDosei_UnkMotion4_Anim.
smart_moves.why_it_matters: This is consistent with the loaded matching-tactics standard: stack padding should be narrow and removable when the source shape can match without it.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report records itDosei_UnkMotion4_Anim reaches 100.00%

decomp_lessons.lesson: When generated source contains expanded jobj.h rotation/assert bodies, first try canonical HSD_JObjSetRotation*WithMtxDirty helpers or a narrow local wrapper around them before preserving copied inline bodies.
scope: item TUs with expanded rotation setters
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report records the targeted item function match

decomp_lessons.lesson: Separating a repeated arithmetic expression from the side-effecting setter can make it easier to choose between ordinary setters and WithMtxDirty setters per call site.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: HSD_JObjSetRotationY and HSD_JObjSetRotationYWithMtxDirty call-site replacements

decomp_lessons.lesson: Do not assume GET_ITEM(gobj) is always the matching access form; this PR used direct gobj->user_data in several item callbacks.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: GET_ITEM(gobj) changed to gobj->user_data in selected functions

naming_conventions.name: itDosei_UnkMotion4_GetAnimSpeed / itDosei_UnkMotion4_GetAttrs

follow_up_queries: Find other item TUs with expanded jobj.h rotation setters and __assert("jobj.h") bodies that could use HSD_JObjSetRotation*WithMtxDirty helpers.
Search for remaining PAD_STACK uses in src/melee/it/items and compare against bot/object-diff evidence before accepting them.
Search for sdata2_ordering-style volatile literal helpers to determine whether they are isolated matching artifacts or recurring data-ordering work.
Search for comments containing 'permuterslop' and route them for cleanup review or documentation as narrow matching exceptions.
Compare item callback access patterns using GET_ITEM(gobj) versus gobj->user_data in nearby matched item files.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Prefer canonical jobj rotation helpers over copied jobj.h assert bodies when they express the same source operation
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2611 replaced local expanded HSD_JObj rotation setter bodies containing jobj.h asserts with calls to HSD_JObjSetRotationX/Y/ZWithMtxDirty and a local zeroing wrapper, while the bot report recorded the targeted itdosei function match. This supports a reusable lesson, consistent with the loaded header-inlines standard, for item code with expanded jobj rotation inlines.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removed itDosei_SetRotX/SetRotY/SetRotZ expanded assert helpers

accepted_candidate_records.title: Split pure facing-angle calculation from rotation setter selection
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2611 introduced itDosei_FacingAngle to compute the angle and then used either HSD_JObjSetRotationY or HSD_JObjSetRotationYWithMtxDirty at individual call sites. This is a narrowly evidenced matching tactic for cases where arithmetic is shared but side-effecting setter shape varies.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: repeated HSD_JObjSetRotationY and HSD_JObjSetRotationYWithMtxDirty replacements

source_update_candidates.title: Document volatile literal ordering helpers as high-risk data-ordering exceptions if recurring
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2611 added sdata2_ordering with volatile f32/f64 constants and the bot report recorded .sdata2 improvement. Because loaded standards already warn against fake data anchors and literal/data ownership churn, this should not be promoted directly; curator may search for recurrence and, if justified, document that such helpers require explicit section/data evidence and narrow regression checks.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential recurring tactic, but PR evidence is insufficient to treat volatile literal helpers as accepted style.

source_update_candidates.title: Clarify handling of explicit permuter-shaped dummy branches
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2611 contains a source comment '// permuterslop' and a nested empty null-check block in itDosei_UnkMotion4_Anim. The bot report supports the final match, but the loaded standards already require evidence for dummy locals/control-flow tactics. Curator may consider adding a warning/example that such blocks are narrow exceptions requiring verifier evidence and cleanup notes.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: '// permuterslop' nested if (jobj == NULL) block
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR provides a concrete example of a questionable but merged matching artifact; it should be routed for standards review rather than accepted broadly.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that volatile sdata2_ordering helpers are generally acceptable; the PR only shows one use and a bot .sdata2 improvement.
Do not infer that the 'permuterslop' dummy null-check block is endorsed style; treat it as a narrow matching artifact with limited evidence.
Do not claim all changed functions in itdosei.c matched individually; the loaded bot report names only itDosei_UnkMotion4_Anim as a new match.
Do not invent human reviewer feedback; loaded PR slice has zero review comments and no human issue comments beyond the PR body.
